title: ABC doesn't retime when _DFF_PP1_ cells are used, but does retime when _DFF_P_ cells are used
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.54+29 (git sha1 7b0c1fe49, clang++ 18.1.8 -fPIC -O3)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Consider `test_top.v`:
```
module top (
    input wire clk,
    input wire rst,
    input wire [7:0] data_in,
    output wire [7:0] data_out
);

parameter WIDTH = 31;

reg [WIDTH-1:0] state_reg = 0;
wire [WIDTH-1:0] state_next;

test_module #(
    .WIDTH(WIDTH),
    .DATA_WIDTH(8)
) test_module (
    .data_in(data_in),
    .state_in(state_reg),
    .data_out(data_out),
    .state_out(state_next)
);

always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
    if (rst)
        state_reg <= {WIDTH{1'b1}};
    else
        state_reg <= state_next;
end

endmodule

module test_module #(
    parameter WIDTH = 31,
    parameter DATA_WIDTH = 8,
)
(
    input  wire [DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_in,
    input  wire [WIDTH-1:0] state_in,
    output wire [DATA_WIDTH-1:0] data_out,
    output wire [WIDTH-1:0] state_out
);

    assign data_out = state_in & data_in;
    assign state_out = data_in | state_in;
endmodule
```

`ff_map.v`:
```
module \$_DFF_PP1_ (input C, D, R, output Q);
    \$_DFF_P_ _TECHMAP_REPLACE_.custom_FF_replace_1 (.C(C), .D(D), .Q(Q));
endmodule
```

And `synth.ys`:

```
read_verilog test_top.v

synth -top top
opt -full
flatten
opt -full
clean -purge

#techmap -map ff_map.v

abc -dff -markgroups -script "+strash; zero; &get -n; print_latch; &fraig -x; &put; scorr; dc2; retime -M 5 -D 1 -o -v; strash; &get -n; &dch -f; &nf -D 1; &put"
```

Run `yosys synth.ys | tee yosys.log` to create `yosys.log`.

`_DFF_PP1_` cells are synthesized after `synth -top top`.

**Inside yosys.log, during the "7.3.1. Executing ABC." step, note that there are warnings saying that "The current network is combinational.", and "The network is combinational (run "fraig" or "fraig_sweep")."**

**No retiming is performed during that step.**

However, let's use `_DFF_P_` instead of `_DFF_PP1_`. If you uncomment `techmap -map ff_map.v` from `synth.ys`, then those warnings disappear, and ABC retimes successfully (note that `techmap -map ff_map.v` replaces each `_DFF_PP1_` with `_DFF_P_`).

According to `techlibs/common/simcells.v`, `_DFF_P_` is a "A positive edge D-type flip-flop", and `_DFF_PP1_` is a `A positive edge D-type flip-flop with positive polarity set`.

### Expected Behavior

ABC retimes the circuit, regardless if the flip-flop is `_DFF_PP1_` or `_DFF_P_`.


### Actual Behavior

ABC doesn't retime the circuit if the flip-flop is `_DFF_PP1_`.

I've copied part of `yosys.log` here:

```
7.3.1. Executing ABC.
Running ABC command: "<yosys-exe-dir>/yosys-abc" -s -f <abc-temp-dir>/abc.script 2>&1
ABC: ABC command line: "source <abc-temp-dir>/abc.script".
ABC: 
ABC: + read_blif <abc-temp-dir>/input.blif 
ABC: + read_library <abc-temp-dir>/stdcells.genlib 
ABC: + strash 
ABC: + zero 
ABC: Warning: The current network is combinational.
ABC: + &get -n 
ABC: + print_latch 
ABC: The network is combinational.
ABC: + &fraig -x 
ABC: + &put 
ABC: + scorr 
ABC: Warning: The network is combinational (run "fraig" or "fraig_sweep").
ABC: + dc2 
ABC: + retime -M 5 -D 1 -o -v 
ABC: + strash 
ABC: + &get -n 
ABC: + &dch -f 
ABC: + &nf -D 1 
ABC: + &put 
ABC: + write_blif <abc-temp-dir>/output.blif 
```
code_blocks:

opt -full
flatten
opt -full
clean -purge

#techmap -map ff_map.v

abc -dff -markgroups -script "+strash; zero; &get -n; print_latch; &fraig -x; &put; scorr; dc2; retime -M 5 -D 1 -o -v; strash; &get -n; &dch -f; &nf -D 1; &put"

Running ABC command: "<yosys-exe-dir>/yosys-abc" -s -f <abc-temp-dir>/abc.script 2>&1

ABC:
